rootCauseClusters.name: Rate Limit Window Miscalculation
rootCauseClusters.description: 5-hour session rate limit sums entire session lifetime output tokens instead of only tokens from messages within the 5-hour window. For long-running sessions, this massively over-reports usage.

rootCauseClusters.name: Race-Unsafe Initialization
rootCauseClusters.description: SessionWatcher starts before initial parse completes. setInitialBuckets overwrites watcher's in-progress work. mergeTimeBuckets mutates source operand via shallow Map copy. Combined: data loss or double-counting during activation.

rootCauseClusters.name: Lifecycle Management Gaps
rootCauseClusters.description: deactivate() does nothing (no timer cleanup, no watcher disposal). globalState offset keys grow unbounded with no pruning. Combined: resource leaks and gradual performance degradation.

rootCauseClusters.name: Unbounded External Calls
rootCauseClusters.description: fetchApiUsage fires on every file change event with no throttle, cache TTL, or deduplication. Active sessions produce dozens of API calls per minute.

rootCauseClusters.name: Configuration Brittleness
rootCauseClusters.description: Strict Zod schema rejects partial pricing overrides. Ambiguous utilization normalization misclassifies over-limit values. Missing pricing for newer models. Combined: silent miscalculation of costs and percentages.

rootCauseClusters.name: Offset Accounting Drift
rootCauseClusters.description: Incremental parser assumes 1-byte line endings (+1). On CRLF files, offset drifts by 1 byte per line, causing re-reads or missed data on subsequent parses.

findings.description: 5-hour session rate limit sums agg.outputTokens (lifetime total) for any session with lastMessage in last 5 hours. For an 8-hour session with 100K total output, all 100K is counted even though only ~40K may be within the window.
findings.suggestion: Store per-message timestamps and output tokens, or maintain a separate time-windowed aggregation. Alternatively, iterate individual records with timestamps within the 5hr window instead of using session-level aggregates.

findings.description: sessionWatcher.start() at line 229 begins processing file events immediately. performInitialParse() at line 413 runs async. During the race window, watcher processes files from offset 0 and advances offsets. setInitialBuckets at line 582 then overwrites currentBuckets, losing watcher's incremental work. Records parsed by watcher but overwritten by setInitialBuckets are never re-read (offsets already advanced).
findings.suggestion: Defer watcher.start() until after performInitialParse completes and setInitialBuckets is called. Or: have performInitialParse also update offset tracker so watcher doesn't re-read.

findings.description: mergeTimeBuckets creates Maps with new Map(a.session) which shallow-copies entries. The mergeBucket function then mutates targetAgg (lines 108-113) via +=, which mutates the original objects from operand 'a'. Combined with TOCTOU in handleFileChange, this silently corrupts the pre-merge state.
findings.suggestion: Deep-copy AggregatedUsage objects when creating the target Map: target.set(key, { ...existingAgg }) before mutating. Or restructure mergeBucket to create new objects instead of mutating.

findings.description: deactivate() only logs a message. It never calls sessionWatcher.dispose() to clear debounce timers. Pending setTimeout callbacks at sessionWatcher.ts:116 fire after disposal, potentially writing to disposed globalState.
findings.suggestion: Call sessionWatcher.dispose() in deactivate(). Also dispose burnRateTracker, credentialsWatcher. Set module-level refs to null.

findings.description: bytesRead += Buffer.byteLength(line, 'utf8') + 1 assumes 1-byte line endings (\n). On Windows CRLF files, the line ending is 2 bytes (\r\n). After N lines, offset drifts by N bytes. Next incremental parse starts mid-line, silently skipping or duplicating records.
findings.suggestion: Detect actual line ending size from the file, or use file stat to compute actual bytes consumed: newOffset = actualStart + (fileSize - actualStart) after reading to EOF. Or track bytes via stream events instead of line-by-line calculation.

findings.description: handleFileChange is async. Two concurrent calls (different files) both read this.currentBuckets, await parseIncremental (yields to event loop), then both write back. Second write overwrites first merge. Result: intermittent data loss.
findings.suggestion: Add a serial processing queue (e.g., chain promises) so handleFileChange calls execute sequentially, not concurrently.

findings.description: fetchApiUsage fires inside the SessionWatcher onUpdate callback on every file change. During active multi-agent sessions, this produces dozens of API calls per minute. No throttle, no dedup, no cache TTL.
findings.suggestion: Add a lastApiFetch timestamp and minimum interval (e.g., 60 seconds). Skip fetch if interval hasn't elapsed. Alternatively, move API fetch to a separate periodic timer.

findings.description: ModelPricingSchema.parse(override) requires all 5 fields. Users providing partial overrides (e.g., only outputPerMillion) get their entire override silently rejected. Warning goes to output channel nobody reads.
Every JSONL file gets a fileOffset:<path> key in globalState. Keys accumulate forever with no pruning. After months of use, thousands of stale keys degrade globalState performance.
findings.suggestion: Add periodic pruning: on activation, check if tracked files still exist. Remove keys for missing files. Or add a TTL/max-keys limit.

findings.description: parseAllSessions processes files sequentially with no parallelism, no progress indicator, no cancellation token. Users with 100+ files experience 10-30s freeze on activation.
findings.suggestion: Add a CancellationToken. Batch files into parallel groups (e.g., 10 concurrent). Report progress to status bar. Consider processing only recent files initially.

findings.description: OAuthCredentials interface declares expiresAt but it is never checked. Expired tokens produce 401 responses, silently falling back to JSONL-only mode. No user notification that API data is unavailable due to expired auth.
findings.suggestion: Check expiresAt before using the token. If expired, log a user-visible warning or attempt refresh using refreshToken.

findings.description: parseWindow normalizes utilization with 'raw.utilization > 1 ? raw.utilization / 100 : raw.utilization'. If API returns 1.5 (150% = over limit), this becomes 0.015 (1.5%). User sees near-zero usage when actually over limit.
findings.suggestion: Document the API's actual return format. If fractions (0.0-1.0+), never divide. If percentages (0-100+), always divide. Remove the ambiguous heuristic.

findings.description: Unknown models fall back to claude-sonnet-4-5 pricing. Opus models (5x more expensive) get Sonnet pricing. New model releases silently produce wrong costs.
findings.suggestion: Attempt fuzzy matching on model name prefixes (claude-opus -> opus pricing, claude-sonnet -> sonnet pricing). Log prominently when fallback is used.

findings.description: Every assistant line is JSON.parse'd twice: once at line 45 to check type, again inside parseAssistantMessage at schemas.ts:75. ~2x CPU cost on the parsing hot path.
findings.suggestion: Pass the already-parsed object to parseAssistantMessage instead of the raw string. Change schema validation to accept parsed JSON.

findings.description: getSelectedPlan() is duplicated: once as a closure in activate() (line 83) and again in performInitialParse() (line 441). Identical logic that could diverge silently.
findings.suggestion: Extract to a module-level function that reads from module-level detectedTier. Remove the duplicate.

findings.description: extensionVersion hardcoded as '0.1.0' but package.json version is '1.0.0'. Exported data has wrong version metadata.
findings.suggestion: Read version from package.json or vscode.extensions.getExtension().

findings.description: Uses console.warn/console.error instead of the structured Logger used everywhere else. Bypasses the output channel logging system.
findings.suggestion: Accept a Logger parameter or use a module-level Logger.

findings.description: Uses require('node:path') and require('node:os') inline instead of top-level imports. Also duplicates getClaudeProjectsDir() logic.
findings.suggestion: Import getClaudeProjectsDir from utils/paths.ts and use it.

planAttacks.attack: Token limits are community guesses. If wrong, percentage displays are misleading. Users can't verify correctness without hitting actual limits.
planAttacks.defense: API data provides accurate percentages when available. refineLimitEstimate learns from 429 events, ratcheting estimates downward. Settings allow manual override.

planAttacks.attack: Incremental offset tracking is fragile. Any parsing bug that produces wrong offsets permanently skews future reads until manual reset.
planAttacks.defense: Offset reset exists via Clear Data command. File truncation detection (offset > fileSize) resets to 0. But CRLF drift has no self-correction.

planAttacks.attack: The API endpoint has no documented stability. Format changes break parsing silently.
planAttacks.defense: parseWindow returning null is graceful fallback. But users get no warning that API data disappeared.

planAttacks.attack: No data validation on deserialization from globalState. Corrupt stored data could crash.
planAttacks.defense: try/catch in usageStore.ts handles deserialization errors and returns null. But no per-field validation on restored data.
